question_text: A seesaw has two children sitting on it. Child A (40 kg) sits 1.5 m from the pivot on the left. Child B (30 kg) sits on the right side. Where should Child B sit to balance the seesaw, assuming gravity pulls down at 10 N/kg?
explanation: Child A creates a moment of 40 × 10 × 1.5 = 600 Nm. For balance, Child B must create the same moment: 30 × 10 × d = 600, so d = 2 m. Child B must sit 2 m from the pivot on the opposite side.
